title: Delicate Caramelized Canadian Goats Cheese Tart
description: A sweet and savory Canadian dish featuring caramelized goats cheese, this tart is perfect for a kid-friendly snack or dessert, and is also ayurvedic-sattvic-friendly due to its use of natural ingredients and gentle cooking methods. The resinous and glassy-jelly texture of the caramelized cheese is a delight to eat.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
Roll out the pie crust and place it in a tart pan with a removable bottom.
In a small bowl, mix together the caramel sauce, honey, salt, and black pepper.
Spread the caramel mixture evenly over the pie crust, leaving a 1-inch border around the edges.
Crumble the goats cheese over the caramel mixture, making sure to stay within the border.
Sprinkle the chopped fresh thyme over the goats cheese.
Drizzle the melted unsalted butter over the thyme.
Bake the tart in the preheated oven for 25-30 minutes, or until the crust is golden brown and the cheese is caramelized.
Remove the tart from the oven and let it cool completely on a wire rack.
Serve the tart warm or at room temperature, cut into small slices.
